\begin{questions}

\question 
Let $A = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & -1 \\ 2 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.
\begin{parts}
\part[2] Find a vector satisfying $\|\mathbf{x}\|_\infty = 1$ and $\|A\mathbf{x}\|_\infty = \|A\|_\infty$.
\part[2] Find a vector satisfying $\|\mathbf{x}\|_1 = 1$ and $\|A\mathbf{x}\|_1 = \|A\|_1$.
\part[6] Find a vector satisfying $\|\mathbf{x}\|_2 = 1$ such that $\|A\mathbf{x}\|_2 = \|A\|_2$. 
\end{parts}

\begin{solution}
\textbf{(a)} $\|A\|_\infty = \max\{1, 2\} = 2$ (maximum absolute row sum).

For $\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \end{bmatrix}$, we have $A\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} x_2 \\ -2x_1 \end{bmatrix}$.

We need $\max\{|x_2|, 2|x_1|\} = 2$ with $\max\{|x_1|, |x_2|\} = 1$. This requires $|x_1| = 1$.

\textbf{Answer:} $\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} \pm 1 \\ x_2 \end{bmatrix}$ where $|x_2| \leq 1$.

\textbf{(b)} $\|A\|_1 = \max\{2, 1\} = 2$ (maximum absolute column sum).

For $\mathbf{x} = \mathbf{e}_1 = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 0 \end{bmatrix}$: $A\mathbf{e}_1 = \begin{bmatrix} 0 \\ -2 \end{bmatrix}$, so $\|A\mathbf{e}_1\|_1 = 2$.

\textbf{Answer:} $\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 0 \end{bmatrix}$.

\textbf{(c)} Parameterize $\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} \cos\theta \\ \sin\theta \end{bmatrix}$. Then $A\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} \sin\theta \\ -2\cos\theta \end{bmatrix}$.

\[
\|A\mathbf{x}\|_2^2 = \sin^2\theta + 4\cos^2\theta = 4 - 3\sin^2\theta
\]

This is maximized when $\sin\theta = 0$, giving $\|A\|_2 = 2$.

\textbf{Answer:} $\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} \pm 1 \\ 0 \end{bmatrix}$.
\end{solution}

\newpage 
\question[10] Find condition number of $A_3 = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$ in $\infty$-norm and 1-norm.

\begin{solution}
\textbf{Computing $\|A_3\|_\infty$:}

Maximum absolute row sum: $\|A_3\|_\infty = \max\{2, 2, 1\} = 2$.

\textbf{Computing $A_3^{-1}$:}

Since $A_3$ is upper triangular with ones on the diagonal, we compute the inverse:
\[
A_3^{-1} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}
\]

\textbf{Computing $\|A_3^{-1}\|_\infty$:}

Maximum absolute row sum: $\|A_3^{-1}\|_\infty = \max\{3, 2, 1\} = 3$.

\textbf{Condition number in $\infty$-norm:}
\[
\kappa_\infty(A_3) = \|A_3\|_\infty \|A_3^{-1}\|_\infty = 2 \cdot 3 = 6
\]

\textbf{Computing $\|A_3\|_1$:}

Maximum absolute column sum: $\|A_3\|_1 = \max\{1, 2, 2\} = 2$.

\textbf{Computing $\|A_3^{-1}\|_1$:}

Maximum absolute column sum: $\|A_3^{-1}\|_1 = \max\{1, 2, 3\} = 3$.

\textbf{Condition number in 1-norm:}
\[
\kappa_1(A_3) = \|A_3\|_1 \|A_3^{-1}\|_1 = 2 \cdot 3 = 6
\]

\textbf{Answer:} $\kappa_\infty(A_3) = \kappa_1(A_3) = 6$.
\end{solution}

\end{questions}
